claim: STAR reports ~250x time savings and 2x space savings vs "a fixed-connectivity, fully fault-tolerant scheme" (arXiv:2509.18294; PRX Quantum 7, 020343, 2026)
Heterogeneous NA/SC architecture reports 752x speedup vs NA-only baselines and >10x fewer qubits vs SC-only systems (arXiv:2601.10144)
Architecture reports 138x fewer physical qubits "under detailed accounting" (arXiv:2604.06319)
High-rate STAR reports 20-40x space-time cost reduction vs the previous best-in-class STAR architecture (arXiv:2606.25011)
Tour de Gross reports ~10x qubit efficiency vs equivalent surface-code architectures (arXiv:2506.03094)
Routing codes report ~8x overhead reduction vs the surface code at equal logical error (arXiv:2606.25330)
Exhibit 0: the "roughly 1,500 to 3,000 qubits" figure circulating in trade coverage of arXiv:2509.18294 does not appear anywhere in the paper, which states ~10,000 physical qubits at p=1e-3

B2: conditional on B1 resolving TRUE, the platform of that first demonstration is neutral atoms
